docs.title: Hello, World! 👋
docs.text: Welcome to this site, built with MkDocs Material. It is responsive, accessible, and supports light & dark mode out of the box.

docs.title: What you'll find here
docs.text:
✍️ Blog

Articles, tutorials, and updates posted regularly. Dive in and explore.

📬 Contact

Have a question or just want to say hi? Drop me a message anytime.

🌗 Light & Dark Mode

Toggle between light and dark themes using the button in the header.

docs.title: About this site
docs.text:
This site was built using MkDocs with the Material theme. Key features include:

  • 🎨 Gradient colour theme throughout the design
  • 📱 Fully responsive — looks great on any device
  • ♿ Accessible — semantic HTML, ARIA labels, and high-contrast focus indicators
  • 🌗 Light / Dark mode — automatically detects your OS preference, switchable any time
  • ✍️ Blog — a dedicated section for posts, categorised and archived automatically
  • 📬 Contact — a clean contact form to get in touch

Feel free to explore the site using the navigation bar above, or jump straight to the blog or contact page.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
Fill out the form below and I'll get back to you as soon as possible.

Your Name * Email Address * Subject Message * Send Message 📨

Form submissions

This form uses FormSubmit for static-site form handling. Replace your-email@example.com in contact.md with your actual email address to start receiving messages.

docs.title: Blog
docs.text: Welcome to the blog! Browse the latest posts below, or use the archive and category filters on the left to find topics that interest you.

docs.title: Getting Started with MkDocs Material 📚
docs.text: MkDocs Material is one of the best ways to build a fast, beautiful, and accessible website from Markdown files. In this post, I'll walk you through a minimal setup and highlight some of the most useful features.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
You'll need Python 3.8 or higher. Install MkDocs and the Material theme with:

pip install mkdocs-material

docs.title: Basic configuration
docs.text:
Create a mkdocs.yml file in your project root:

site_name: My Site
theme:
  name: material
  palette:
    - scheme: default
      toggle:
        icon: material/brightness-7
        name: Switch to dark mode
    - scheme: slate
      toggle:
        icon: material/brightness-4
        name: Switch to light mode

docs.title: Adding pages
docs.text:
All your content lives inside the docs/ folder. The nav: key in mkdocs.yml controls what appears in the navigation:

nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Blog: blog/index.md
  - Contact: contact.md

docs.title: Enabling the blog
docs.text:
Material's built-in blog plugin makes it easy to manage posts:

plugins:
  - search
  - blog

Place posts inside docs/blog/posts/ with a date in the front matter, and they'll be automatically indexed, archived, and categorised.

docs.title: Deploying to GitHub Pages
docs.text:
Add this GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/deploy.yml) and push to main — your site will be live at https://<username>.github.io/<repo>/ within minutes:

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'
      - run: pip install mkdocs-material
      - run: mkdocs gh-deploy --force

That's the essentials! The official documentation covers every feature in depth — it's worth bookmarking.

docs.title: Welcome to My Blog 🎉
docs.text: Hello and welcome! I'm thrilled you've found your way here. This post is a quick introduction to what this blog is all about and what you can expect from it going forward.

docs.title: What this blog is about
docs.text: This blog is a space for sharing ideas, tutorials, thoughts on technology, and anything else that sparks curiosity. Whether you're a developer, a designer, or just someone who enjoys reading about the web — there's something here for you.

docs.title: What to expect
docs.text:
Here's a rough sense of the kinds of content I plan to post:

  • 🛠️ Tutorials — step-by-step guides on building things with modern tools
  • 💡 Tips & tricks — short, actionable pieces of advice
  • 📖 Deep dives — longer explorations of topics I find fascinating
  • 🗺️ Updates — news about this site and what I'm working on

docs.title: Why now?
docs.text:
Everyone has ideas worth sharing. Writing is one of the best ways to clarify thinking, and publishing is one of the best ways to share what you've learned. This blog is my commitment to do both more consistently.

Thanks for stopping by. Feel free to get in touch if you have any thoughts or suggestions — I'd love to hear from you!
